title: strychnos johnsonii
intro: Strychnos johnsonii, also known as the African poison nut or Johnson's strychnine tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its seeds, which contain a toxic alkaloid called strychnine. However, the seeds also have health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In traditional herbal medicine, Strychnos johnsonii seeds are often prepared by boiling or drying to make infusions, decoctions, or powders for medicinal use.
